MINI MINUTES FOR APR-23-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND GEORGE GASCON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 [RETALIATION (LABOR CODE SEC. 1102.5-WHISTLEBLOWER)] IS OVERRULED AS TO CCSF AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO GEORGE GASCON. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THE VIOLATION OF A STATE STATUTE/S AND THE CLAIM CAN ONLY BE PLEADED AGAINST THE EMPLOYER. THE DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 [WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY (LABOR CODE SEC. 1102.5)] IS OVERRULED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES A VIOLATION OF LABOR CODE SEC. 1102.5. THE DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION 3 [WRONGFUL TERMINATION IN VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY (LABOR CODE SEC. 232.5] IS OVERRULED. THE DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION 4 [NEGLIGENT SUPERVISION], 5 [INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS], AND 6 [NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS] ARE SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CCSF. THEY ARE COMMON LAW CAUSES OF ACTION THAT ARE NOT ACTIONABLE UNDER THE GOVERNMENT CLAIMS ACT. GEORGE GASCON'S DEMURRERS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 5 AND 6 ARE SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. SEE MIKLOSY V. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (2008) 44 CAL.4TH 876, 902. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: PAULA SHI #13358.

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joel Siegel, Esq., 415.777.5547, for plaintiff Paul Brennan.
Arthur Hartinger, Esq., 415.421.3711, for defendants City and County of San Francisco and George Gascon.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling re: DEFENDANTs CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and GEORGE GASCON's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer to cause of action 1 [retaliation (Labor Code sec. 1102.5-whistleblower)] is overruled as to CCSF and sustained without leave to amend as to George Gascon. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the violation of a state statute/s and the claim can only be pleaded against the employer. The demurrer to cause of action 2 [wrongful termination in violation of public policy (Labor Code sec. 1102.5)] is overruled because plaintiff sufficiently alleges a violation of Labor Code sec. 1102.5. The demurrer to cause of action 3 [wrongful termination in violation of public policy (Labor Code sec. 232.5] is overruled. The demurrer to causes of action 4 [negligent supervision], 5 [intentional infliction of emotional distress], and 6 [negligent infliction of emotional distress] are sustained without leave to amend as to CCSF. They are common law causes of action that are not actionable under the Government Claims Act. George Gascon's demurrers to causes of action 5 and 6 are sustained without leave to amend. See Miklosy v. the Regents of the University of California (2008) 44 Cal.4th 876, 902. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Paula Shi #13358.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-23-2014 Continued To May-30-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO and GEORGE GASCON'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to May 30, 2014 to give both parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Defendant must deliver courtesy copies of the Complaint and Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 23, 2014 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Judge: A. James Robertson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
